$H(s, 0^k 1 0^{L-1-k})$:
We flip $s[k]$ if it is '0'. (Cost $[s_k = '0']$).
We flip $s[i]$ for $i \ne k$ if it is '1'. (Cost $\sum_{i \ne k} [s_i = '1']$).
Let $N_1$ be total ones in $s$.
$\sum_{i \ne k} [s_i = '1'] = N_1 - [s_k = '1']$.
$C_{1\_at\_k} = [s_k = '0'] + N_1 - [s_k = '1']$.

If $s_k = '0'$: $C = 1 + N_1 - 0 = N_1 + 1$.
If $s_k = '1'$: $C = 0 + N_1 - 1 = N_1 - 1$.

To minimize $C_{1\_at\_k}$, we should choose $k$ such that $s_k = '1'$.
Minimum $C_{1\_at\_k} = N_1 - 1$ (if $N_1 \ge 1$). If $N_1=0$, this case is $s'=0^L$, cost $C_0=0$.

If $N_1 > 0$, the minimum cost from this set is $N_1 - 1$.
